name: Auth0 API
what_it_does: Enterprise identity platform providing authentication, authorization, and user management via OAuth2/OIDC with support for social logins, MFA, SSO, and a management API for user/application administration.
use_cases: Adding authentication to apps without building it from scratch
Managing users and roles via agent-driven Management API
Enforcing MFA policies and monitoring login events
not_for: Teams needing fully self-hosted identity (use Keycloak instead)
Simple API key management without user identity
Organizations with very tight budgets at scale (MAU pricing gets expensive)
best_when: An agent needs to manage user identities, enforce auth policies, or handle M2M tokens in an enterprise app already using Auth0.
avoid_when: You need self-hosted identity or your user count makes MAU pricing prohibitive.

auth.notes: Management API uses machine-to-machine OAuth2 client credentials flow. Authentication API endpoints are public. Fine-grained scopes required for most management operations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Essential from $35/month, Professional from $240/month, Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: Free tier suitable for development and small apps. MAU-based pricing can become expensive at scale. Enterprise features (fine-grained auth, custom domains) require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Management API: 2 requests/second burst, varies by endpoint and plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear JSON error responses with error codes and descriptions. Management API errors are well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation is idempotent by email. Role and permission assignments use upsert semantics in some endpoints.
known_agent_gotchas: Management API tokens expire in 24h by default - agents must refresh or use short-lived tokens carefully
Rate limits on Management API are strict (2 req/sec) and easy to hit when bulk-managing users
Tenant-level rate limits differ from endpoint-level limits - check both
User search uses Lucene syntax which is non-obvious for agents
Creating users via Management API bypasses email verification flows
